findings.title: Any approved NFT operator can force callback execution and drain a position's withdrawable value

best_agent_match.title: Any approved NFT operator can drain a position by calling the callback directly

findings.title: User-supplied swap data is an arbitrary-call primitive while V3Utils temporarily owns the user's position

best_agent_match.title: Swap data is an unrestricted arbitrary-call primitive while V3Utils owns the user's position

findings.title: Residual position-manager allowances can permanently brick zero-first ERC20 flows across the deployment

best_agent_match.title: Residual position-manager allowances can permanently brick zero-first ERC20s for all users

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can increase liquidity on any Uniswap V3 position
rejected_candidates.reason: By design: the caller only contributes their own assets to someone else's position and cannot extract value or create protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can mint a new position with any parameters
rejected_candidates.reason: By design: public minting uses the caller's own funds and does not move or lock third-party assets.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked swap when amountIn is zero
rejected_candidates.reason: `_swap` is gated by `amountIn > 0`, so zero-input `swapData` is inert. The real issue is arbitrary external execution during non-zero swaps, captured in F-002.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline validation in execute callback
rejected_candidates.reason: No standalone exploit: the price-sensitive Uniswap subcalls already receive `deadline`, and stale execution only becomes harmful through the authorization flaw captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation of recipient address
rejected_candidates.reason: Using `address(0)` only burns funds chosen by the caller or by an attacker already covered by F-001; it is not an independent vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing slippage protection in changeRange new position mint
rejected_candidates.reason: `CHANGE_RANGE` does mint with zero mins, but leftovers are returned; absent another bug, this is strategy quality/user choice rather than realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No access control on swap function
rejected_candidates.reason: Public swap functionality is intentional; callers expose only their own assets and chosen routing data.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing event for swapAndIncreaseLiquidity input validation failure
rejected_candidates.reason: Operational visibility only; not a security issue.
